title: taraxacum kraettlii
intro: Taraxacum kraettlii, also known as the Alpine dandelion, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to the European Alps. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antioxidant effects. The Alpine dandelion has been traditionally used to support liver and kidney health, as well as to alleviate digestive issues and reduce blood pressure. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal capsules and supplements to harness its potential health benefits.
